info.title: ietf-l2vpn-ntw
info.description:
This YANG module defines a network model for Layer 2 VPN
services.

Copyright (c) 2022 IETF Trust and the persons identified as
authors of the code.  All rights reserved.

Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or
without modification, is permitted pursuant to, and subject
to the license terms contained in, the Revised BSD License
set forth in Section 4.c of the IETF Trust's Legal Provisions
Relating to IETF Documents
(https://trustee.ietf.org/license-info).

This version of this YANG module is part of RFC 9291; see
the RFC itself for full legal notices.

cnc-host.description: host name or ip address of the target CNC Cluster

cnc-port.description: port number of the target CNC Cluster

parameters.name: vpn-service-vpn-id
parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: limit the depth of nodes in response

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: optionally identify specific data nodes in response

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: xpath expression to filter data nodes in response

parameters.name: vpn-service-vpn-id
parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network.

parameters.name: insert
parameters.description: controlling the order when adding new list elements

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: used to specify the insertion point

parameters.name: vpn-service-vpn-id
parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network.

parameters.name: vpn-service-vpn-id
parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network.

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:custom-template.description: Custom templates to apply when device is ready (list)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:iteration.description: Apply custom template multiple times with iteration (list)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:variable.description: Variables to bind when applying template in iteration (list)
Variables to bind when applying template one time (list)

vpn-service-topology.description:
Defines service topology such as
any-to-any, hub-spoke, etc. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:bridge-group.description: Bridge-group is mandatory if vpn-type = mpls-evpn (leaf)

vpn-id.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:custom-template.description: Custom templates to apply when device is ready (list)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:iteration.description: Apply custom template multiple times with iteration (list)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:variable.description: Variables to bind when applying template in iteration (list)
Variables to bind when applying template one time (list)

vpn-node-id.description: Sets the identifier of the VPN node. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:sr-policy.description:
SR Policy for TE mapping.
L2VPN association with SRv6-TE (SR Policy and ODN) is not supported for this release (presence)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:locator.description:
SRv6 locator name (1-58 chars) at individual evi service level,
leave it empty if evi or global level locator is used. Special characters are not allowed. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:te-service-mapping.description: Mapping of this service to an optional Traffic Engineered path (non-presence)

role.description: Role of the VPN node in the VPN. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:vpn-policies.description:
VPN service policies.  'vpn-policies' contains references
to the import and export policies to be associated with
the VPN service. (non-presence)

mac-learning-mode.description:
Indicates through which plane MAC
addresses are advertised. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:vpn-target.description:
RTs.  AND/OR operations may be defined based on the
assigned RTs. (list)

evpn-policies.description:
Includes a set of EVPN policies such
as those related to handling MAC
addresses. (non-presence)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:duration.description:
<1-1440> Duration of operations expressed in minutes.
The 'forever' option will try to schedule infinitely if supported; otherwise,
maximum number of allowed minutes will be used (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:interval.description:
<1|2|3|4|5|6|8|9|10|12|15|16|18|20|24|30|32|36|40|45|48|60|80|90>
Interval between operations expressed in minute. Must be a factor of 1440.
Only applicable for Cisco XR devices. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:icc-based-id.description: icc-based SMAN ID allocated by resource-manager id-allocator (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:number-id.description: number SMAN ID allocated by resource-manager id-allocator (leaf)

message-period.description: Defines the interval between OAM messages. (leaf)

qos-profile.description:
QoS profile.
Can be a standard or customized
profile. (list)

direction.description:
The direction to which the QoS profile
is applied. (leaf)

mtu.description:
Layer 2 MTU; it is also known as the maximum
transmission unit or maximum frame size. (leaf)

description.description: A textual description of the VPN network access. (leaf)

tag-1.description:
A first tag to be used for push or
translate operations.  This tag will be
used as the outermost tag as a result
of the tag operation. (leaf)

tag-operations.description:
Sets the tag manipulation policy for this
VPN network access.  It defines a set of
tag manipulations that allow for the
insertion, removal, or rewriting
of 802.1Q VLAN tags.  These operations are
indicated for the CE-PE direction.
By default, tag operations are symmetric.
As such, the reverse tag operation is
assumed on the PE-CE direction. (non-presence)

encap-type.description:
Tagged interface type.  By default, the
type of the tagged interface is
'priority-tagged'. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:tag-1.description:
A first tag to be used for push or
translate operations. This tag will be
used as the outermost tag as a result
of the tag operation. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:push.description:
Push one tag defined by the
tag-1 leaf. It is
assumed that, absent any policy, the
default value of 0 will be used for
PCP setting. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:tag-operations.description:
Sets the tag manipulation policy for this
VPN network access. It defines a set of
tag manipulations that allow for the
insertion, removal, or rewriting
of 802.1Q VLAN tags. These operations are
indicated for the CE-PE direction.
By default, tag operations are symmetric.
As such, the reverse tag operation is
assumed on the PE-CE direction. (non-presence)

lag-interface.description:
Container of LAG interface attributes
configuration. (non-presence)

l2-termination-point.description:
Specifies a reference to a local Layer 2
termination point such as a Layer 2
sub-interface. (leaf)

connection.description: Container for the bearer and AC. (non-presence)

interface-id.description: Refers to a physical or logical interface. (leaf)

vpn-network-accesses.description: Main container for VPN network accesses. (non-presence)

vpn-nodes.description: Set of VPN nodes that are involved in the L2NM. (non-presence)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:sr-policy-ref.description: List of sr-policies (static or dynamically created) (list)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:discovered-underlay-tran.description: HCO service to underlay transport mapping (non-presence)

instance-type.description:
Indicates a transport instance type.  For example,
it can be a VPN+, an IETF network slice, a virtual
network, etc. (leaf)

transport-instance-id.description:
An optional identifier of the abstract transport
instance. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:profile-name.description:
Please input profile name in following format: '<profile-name> (custom|system)'
 The possible profile names for system are: Gold_L2VPN_ConfigProfile, Gold_L3VPN_ConfigProfile, Silver_L2VPN_ConfigProfile, Silver_L3VPN_ConfigProfile. (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:preservation.description: Preserve/Remove all assurance related historical data after stop monitoring (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:monitoring-state.description: To enable, disable or pause the service assurance monitoring (leaf)

cisco-l2vpn-ntw:rule-name.description:
Please input rule name in following format: '<rule-name> (custom|system)'
 The possible rule names for system are: Rule-L2VPN-NM, Rule-L2VPN-NM-Basic, Rule-L2VPN-NM-P2P, Rule-L2VPN-NM-P2P-Basic, Rule-L2VPN-MP, Rule-L2VPN-MP-Basic, Rule-L3VPN-NM, Rule-L3VPN-NM-Basic. (leaf)

last-change.description:
Indicates the actual date and time of the service status
change. (leaf)

fields.name: fields
fields.description: optionally identify specific data nodes in response

filter.name: filter
filter.description: xpath expression to filter data nodes in response

depth.name: depth
depth.description: limit the depth of nodes in response

insert.name: insert
insert.description: controlling the order when adding new list elements

point.name: point
point.description: used to specify the insertion point

vpn-service-vpn-id.name: vpn-service-vpn-id
vpn-service-vpn-id.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network.
